metadata.name: Cloudflare Zero Trust Tunnel Config
metadata.description: Creates a Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnel ingress configuration for a cloudflared tunnel with a primary hostname and fallback service.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Creates a Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnel configuration for an existing `cloudflared` tunnel.

## Prerequisites
- The target tunnel must already exist in Cloudflare Zero Trust.
- A `cloudflared` connector should already be online so the ingress rules have a live tunnel to attach to.

## Apply
- Render `main.tf`, then run `terraform fmt`, `terraform plan`, and `terraform apply`.
- Review the generated ingress order carefully because Cloudflare evaluates ingress rules from top to bottom.

## Follow-Up
- Test the published hostname after apply and confirm the fallback rule behaves as expected.
- Keep the Docker-side `cloudflared` deployment and the Terraform-side ingress config in sync.

## References
- [Cloudflare Tunnel](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/)
- [Terraform Provider](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudflare/cloudflare/latest/docs/resources/zero_trust_tunnel_cloudflared_config)

items.name: resource_name
items.title: Resource Name
items.description: Terraform resource name (alphanumeric and underscores only)

items.name: ingress_hostname
items.title: Hostname
items.description: Public hostname routed through the tunnel

items.name: ingress_service
items.title: Service URL
items.description: Backend service URL for the hostname

items.name: prevent_destroy
items.title: Prevent resource destruction
items.description: Prevent Terraform from destroying the resource.

items.name: ignore_changes
items.title: Ignore Changes
items.description: Comma-separated list of attributes to ignore changes for
